User Story / Description: Customer, agent, or seller can view complete order and ancillary state
User understands whether one passenger, selected passengers, or all passengers can be changed
Eligible passenger split can be quoted and confirmed safely
User can view fare difference, penalties, taxes, and total payable/refundable amount
Paid seats, baggage, meals, and other extras are transferred, reselected, repriced, or refunded
Customer can see ticket and ancillary refund estimate before cancellation
Seller channels receive consistent servicing APIs with measurable reliability
As a traveler, agent, or seller, I want to retrieve the complete order, so that I can understand itinerary, passengers, fare, payment, and purchased ancillaries before servicing.
Acceptance Criteria: Valid order returns itinerary, passengers, fare, payment, and order items; ancillaries include continuity state; unauthorized access blocked; response includes correlation ID and audit reference.

User Story / Description: As a platform owner, I want secure access control for order retrieval, so that passenger and payment-adjacent information is protected.
Acceptance Criteria: Direct users pass order/passenger validation; agents and sellers pass channel permission checks; failures do not expose PII; failed attempts logged.

User Story / Description: As a traveler or seller, I want to know which servicing actions are available, so that I do not start a flow that cannot be completed.
Acceptance Criteria: Allowed, blocked, and assisted actions are listed; blocked actions show reason and next best action; eligibility shown at order and passenger level.

User Story / Description: As a traveler with a multi-passenger order, I want to select one or more passengers for a change, so that I do not accidentally change the full group itinerary.
Acceptance Criteria: User selects one, multiple, or all passengers; system explains split requirement; assisted handoff created when online flow is unavailable; unchanged passengers are clearly shown.

User Story / Description: As a traveler or seller, I want to see the impact of splitting selected passengers, so that I understand what will happen before a passenger-level change.
Acceptance Criteria: Split quote shows selected and remaining passengers, impacted items, warnings, expiry, and fallback path on failure.

User Story / Description: As a platform owner, I want split confirmation to be idempotent, so that retries do not create duplicate order splits.
Acceptance Criteria: Split confirmation requires idempotency key; retries return original result; original and split order IDs are linked; audit event created.

User Story / Description: As a traveler, I want to view available alternative flights for an eligible change, so that I can choose a new itinerary.
Acceptance Criteria: Alternative search uses original order context and selected passengers; results show flight, cabin, fare compatibility, and indicative price impact; stale results expire.

User Story / Description: As a traveler or seller, I want a complete change quote, so that I can understand fare difference, penalties, taxes, and total payable/refundable amount.
Acceptance Criteria: Quote includes old/new itinerary, fare difference, fee, tax difference, total due/refund, selected passengers, expiry, and ancillary impact.

User Story / Description: As a platform owner, I want change confirmation to be idempotent, so that duplicate retries do not create duplicate charges, refunds, or order updates.
Acceptance Criteria: Confirmation requires valid quote ID and idempotency key; duplicate attempts return existing result; payment/refund linked; order timeline records impacts.

User Story / Description: As a traveler, I want to know what happens to my paid seat after a change, so that I can retain, reselect, or refund it.
Acceptance Criteria: Continuity check identifies same seat availability; equivalent options or refund eligibility shown; price difference included; final state recorded.

User Story / Description: As a traveler, I want to know whether extra baggage moves to my new flight, so that I do not lose paid baggage unexpectedly.
Acceptance Criteria: Rules evaluate route, cabin, passenger, fare, and timing; baggage state shown; additional collection or refund included in quote.

User Story / Description: As a traveler, I want to know when meal preferences need reselection, so that my changed journey still reflects my preferences.
Acceptance Criteria: User is prompted when meal cannot be retained; closed selection window shows reason; summary shows final meal state; timeline updated.

Summary: Generate refund quote for ticket and extras
User Story / Description: As a traveler, I want ticket refund and ancillary refund shown separately, so that I understand what I will receive before cancelling.
Acceptance Criteria: Refund quote displays fare, taxes, penalties, and ancillary refunds separately; quote includes estimated amount, currency, expiry, and reason codes.

User Story / Description: As a traveler or seller, I want to cancel an eligible order after reviewing refund impact, so that I can complete cancellation without contacting support.
Acceptance Criteria: Cancellation requires refund quote ID and idempotency key; status updated; refund request created; failure returns structured error and next action; audit trail created.

Summary: Provide seller servicing API with standard errors
User Story / Description: As a travel seller or OTA partner, I want consistent servicing APIs and error responses, so that I can support customers without switching channels unnecessarily.
Acceptance Criteria: Seller API supports retrieve, eligibility, change quote, ancillary continuity, refund quote, and cancellation; correlation ID and standard errors returned; partner telemetry captured.
